originalAlarm.description: The left operand of '!=' is a garbage value

sanitizedDescription: The left operand of '!=' is a garbage value
model:
(define-fun NDEBUG () Bool
  false)
(define-fun _ASSERT_H_DECLS () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_DISCONTIGMEM () Bool
  true)
reasoning: r is assigned at L18 from pfnnid_map[pfn] (a valid global array read) immediately before the comparison r != 0xff at L19. r is always initialized; the alarm is a false positive. PC includes restrictive library macro !_ASSERT_H_DECLS (assert.h not yet processed in desugared header state). The known VBDB bug (// (6) ERROR at L19) is the assertion always failing because pfnnid_map is filled with 0xff by memset at L53 — a different issue entirely.
